title: GPT
content:
Generative Pretrained Transformers
1. Usage:
Decoder only, with causal mask, trained by next token prediction.
2. Structure:
The overall forward pass reads bottom-to-top as follows:
For each transformer layer, the sub-components also read bottom-to-top:
3. Tokenization:
Use BPE first, returning IDs (integers) in BPE’s vocabulary.
1

Then learn a matrix W to map each ID to an embedding vector.
e
Then learn a position embedding θ .
PE
The final token representation combines both:
θ = W ×ID+θ
token e PE
4. Training:
Next token prediction.
Target: choose θ to maximize likelihood:
= P (x | x ,x ,...,x )
θ t 1 2 t−1
This is equal to minimizing cross-entropy.
The predicted probability is computed as:
p = softmax(h∗W⊤)
θ e
where W⊤ is the transpose of the matrix from tokenization (weight tying), and h∗ is the final hidden
e
state from the transformer layers.
The training loss (negative log-likelihood averaged over the sequence) is:
T
1 (cid:88)
L = − logP (x | x ,x ,...,x )
ce θ t 1 2 t−1
T
t=1
5. Training in Parallel:
Given a sequence:
x = (x ,x ,x ,...)
1 2 3
The targets are the shifted sequence:
y = (x ,x ,...)
2 3
Each prediction is independent from the previous one, so the model is trained in parallel.
2

For one sentence, it uses a matrix where columns are tokens and rows are sentence copies:
 
S S ···
11 12
 
S =  S S ··· 
 21 22 
 
S ··· ···
31
[Diagram 3: Score Matrix with Causal Mask]
The causal mask is applied as S +mask, so that future tokens are masked out:
 
0 −∞ −∞
 
mask =  0 0 −∞ 
 
 
0 0 0
After softmax, entries corresponding to −∞ become exactly zero, ensuring each token only attends
to itself and prior tokens.
The loss is sum then averaged over all the sentences.
6. Encoder-only (BERT) vs Decoder-only (GPT) vs Encoder+Decoder:
(a) Encoder only: bidirectional; better for input understanding.
(b) Decoder only: causal mask; better for free chatting / generation.
(c) Encoder + Decoder: best for conditional generation, e.g. translation.
3
